{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question asks to test the statistical significance of the difference in height between two groups (boys and girls) of the same age. Given that we are comparing means from two independent samples, we should use a t-test. The t-test is appropriate for comparing the means of two groups when the sample sizes are small and the population variances are unknown. The X2 test is used for categorical data, Z-test is used for large sample sizes or known population variance, and F-test is used for comparing variances or in ANOVA. Therefore, the t-test is the most suitable choice.", "answer_choice": "C"}
